summary: Implemented SIM-001 WorldState v0 foundation: branded primitive ID parsers, minimal immutable definitions, separate runtime state tables, deterministic canonical state hash, semantic invariant validator, focused unit/property coverage, sim-core package gates, and architecture static guard coverage for forbidden sim-core authority APIs.

tests_run.result: Vitest ran tests/sim-core-world-state.test.ts: 1 file passed, 7 tests passed.
Architecture boundaries passed. Self-test fixture produced 2 expected violation(s).
architecture:check passed; sim determinism matched Node and Worker-compatible hash 61e0ba1c; root Vitest 2 files/9 tests passed; web Vitest 1 file/3 tests passed.

decisions: Kept SIM-001 minimal: no economy, population, diplomacy, war, historical database, save envelope, RNG, protocol expansion, or production dependency.
WorldState v0 stores canonical hash in meta.stateHash but hash input excludes stored stateHash to avoid self-referential hashing.
Definitions are canonicalized by stable branded numeric IDs; runtime state uses definition IDs and explicit edge/reference fields.
District runtime control uses an explicit uncontrolled variant rather than missing controller fields.
risks: SIM-001 is R3 because it introduces WorldState/hash/invariant foundations; requires systems_architect review before acceptance.

The handoff-requested legacy doc names docs/01-product-brief.md, docs/02-technical-architecture.md, docs/03-simulation-design.md, and docs/04-data-and-save.md do not exist in the current repo; writer read the current equivalent docs and related systems/data/save specs.
Worktree contained project coordination changes and an untracked route file outside the writer implementation scope; they were not modified by the writer pass.
requested_action: Architecture reviewer:审查 SIM-001 implementation for branded ID boundaries, Definition/Runtime State separation, WorldState v0 shape, canonical hash compatibility expectations, semantic invariant coverage, deterministic constraints, and sim-core architecture guard adequacy. Return ACCEPT, REQUEST_CHANGES, or BLOCK.
